rationale.root_cause: They pinpointed program.c:9 (strcpy(buf, argc[1])) copying argv data into a 256-byte stack buffer without length checks and named it a stack-buffer-overflow. They explained the command-line argument can exceed the destination size.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed rejecting inputs where strlen(argv[1]) >= sizeof(buf) before copying and then performing a bounded/valid copy, or using snprintf which preserves NUL-termination. This replaces the unbounded copy with a size-checked, null-terminating approach.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended eliminating raw unbounded string APIs, using size-aware alternatives, and centralizing/validating argv before any copy. This addresses the root cause by removing unsafe string handling from the data path.
